name: Cable Straight-Arm Pullover
resistance_profile: The high cable pulls hardest in the overhead position, loading the lats at full stretch; tension stays on but eases as the handle reaches the thighs.
cues: Pull down and back with the lats
execution_steps: Set the pulley to the highest position, attach a straight or rope handle, grip it with arms nearly straight, and step back to create tension.
Hinge slightly at the hips, brace your core, and let the handle draw your arms up and overhead into a full lat stretch.
Keeping the elbows locked at a soft, fixed angle, drive the handle down toward your thighs using the lats.
Squeeze the lats at the bottom, then return under control to the overhead stretch.
good_when: You want a lat isolation with constant cable tension that loads the stretched position.
You want to pre-exhaust or finish the lats without loading the biceps or lower back.
You need light, joint-friendly loads for higher-rep work.
bad_when: You have limited time and need one movement to build overall back mass (pick a pulldown or row).
You want to move heavy loads for strength, since isolation caps the useful weight.
